# Q2 2026 Revenue Projections

## Seasonal Factors

Q2 is historically Riverside Cafe's strongest quarter due to:

- **Patio season**: The cafe has a 24-seat outdoor patio on NW Thurman Street that opens April 15 (weather permitting). In 2025, patio seating increased dine-in revenue by 35% in Q2 vs. Q1.
- **Portland Rose Festival**: Late May through mid-June brings increased foot traffic to the NW neighborhood. The cafe saw a 20% revenue bump during the 2025 festival.
- **Graduation season**: Portland State University and Lewis & Clark College graduations in May generate catering orders. Three events already booked totaling $8,500.
- **Longer daylight hours**: Extended morning and evening hours from May 1 (opening at 6:30 AM instead of 7:00 AM, closing at 4:00 PM instead of 3:00 PM).

## Revenue Projections

| Month | Projected Revenue | Basis |
|-------|-------------------|-------|
| April | $48,000 | Patio opens mid-month, Easter brunch event ($3,200 booked) |
| May | $55,000 | Full patio, graduation catering, Rose Festival starts |
| June | $57,000 | Peak patio season, Rose Festival, corporate summer events |
| **Q2 Total** | **$160,000** | **23% increase over Q1** |

## Confirmed Bookings (Catering and Events)

- **April 20**: Easter brunch buffet (Riverside Events Co.) — $3,200
- **May 10**: Lewis & Clark College reception — $2,800
- **May 17**: Portland State University department celebration — $3,200
- **May 24**: NW Portland neighborhood block party (coffee cart service) — $1,500
- **June 5**: Corporate team lunch, Elemental Technologies (new client) — $2,500
- **June 14**: Wedding rehearsal dinner (private, Gutierrez family friend) — $4,200
- **Total confirmed catering**: $17,400

## Staffing Adjustments

Maria plans to hire two additional part-time servers for patio season (May through September). Estimated additional payroll cost: $3,200/month. The incremental revenue from patio service ($15,000-$18,000/month) more than justifies the staffing investment.

## Capital Expenditure

The new La Marzocco Linea PB espresso machine (2-group) is scheduled for delivery April 22. Cost: $18,000. Rachel Green confirmed this qualifies for Section 179 immediate expensing in 2026, reducing taxable income by the full $18,000. The existing Nuova Simonelli machine (purchased 2019, fully depreciated) will be sold to a new cafe in Sellwood for $3,500.
